prompt: Use TDD to implement a function that checks whether a string is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards, ignoring case and non-alphanumeric characters).
assertions: A test list or plan is described before implementation starts
Tests cover: empty string, single character, simple palindrome, simple non-palindrome, mixed-case palindrome, palindrome with spaces or punctuation
All tests pass with the final implementation
The refactor phase removes any duplication introduced while making tests pass
The agent runs the test suite after each Red and Green phase
Forcing tests drive each new transformation step; the agent does not jump directly to the complete normalisation logic (e.g. s == s[::-1]) without intermediate tests forcing each addition
Triangulation tests (additional examples of the same rule) are added after the corresponding forcing test is green, not batched into the initial test list
Test names describe the behaviour being verified (e.g. 'single_character_is_a_palindrome', 'ignores_case_when_comparing')
The implementation handles the full normalisation (case folding and non-alphanumeric stripping) only once tests require it — not speculatively
